sourceMovie.title: Everest
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Everest works as a adventure movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The adventure and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Seven Years in Tibet
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Everest clicked for you, Seven Years in Tibet (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Everest?
faq.answer: Try Seven Years in Tibet, Alive, Against the Ice—each shares DNA with Everest while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Everest?
faq.answer: Everest (2015) is best described as Adventure, Drama, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Everest worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
